text:
Once upon a quiet afternoon in a small town, a curious old owl named Oliver perched on his favorite tree overlooking Owl Hollow. Every day, Oliver watched the town's inhabitants go about their routines. From Mrs. Hoot's singing rabbits to puppy chase games at the park, life moved peacefully.

One day, Oliver decided he'd like to hear more stories from the townsfolk. He hooted softly, signaling his intention. A friendly squirrel named Hazel leapt into action.

"Okay," Hazel began, her voice filled with excitement, "there's this old oak tree around the corner. Its branches seem to listen. One tale I heard..."

Oliver blinked in wonder. "Was it a wise old oak?"

Hazel grinned. "Oh yes. They say an acorn once fell upon its bark. Instead of rolling into oblivion, it stopped. The oak grew wise. It taught a deer how to weave intricate nets. It shared knowledge about birds with a robin. It counsel
